classes.name: freighter.url.URL

methods.name: freighter.url.URL.parse
documentation: Parses a URL string into a URL object.

methods.name: freighter.url.URL.replace
documentation: Replace returns a new URL with the specified fields replaced.

methods.name: freighter.url.URL.child
documentation: Creates a new URL with the given path appended to the current path.

methods.name: freighter.url.URL.stringify
documentation: Returns the URL as a string.

URI is a simple class for building and extending URLs.

```
param host

The host name or IP address of the server.

param port

The port number of the server.

param path

The path prefix to use for all requests. Defaults to "".

param protocol

The protocol to use for all requests.
```

functions.name: freighter.exceptions.register_exception
documentation:
Registers a custom error encoder and decoder with the freighter error registry,
which allows
it to be sent over the network.

```
param _encode

A function that takes an exception and returns a string.

param _decode

A function that takes a string and returns an exception.

return

None
```

functions.name: freighter.exceptions.encode_exception
documentation:
Encodes an exception into a payload that can be sent between a freighter server
and client.

```
param exc

The exception to encode.

return

The encoded error payload.
```

functions.name: freighter.exceptions.decode_exception
documentation:
Decode decodes an error payload into an exception. If a custom decoder can be found
for the error type, it will be used. Otherwise, a generic Exception containing the
error data is returned.

```
param encoded

The encoded error payload.

return

The decoded exception.
```

classes.name: freighter.exceptions.ExceptionPayload
documentation:
Error payload is a payload that can be sent between a freighter client and server,
so that it can be decoded into a proper exception by the implementing language.

classes.name: freighter.exceptions.Unreachable
documentation: Raise when a target is unreachable.

classes.name: freighter.exceptions.StreamClosed
documentation: Raised when a stream is closed.

classes.name: freighter.exceptions.EOF
documentation: Raised when a stream is closed.

functions.name: freighter.unary.send_required
documentation: Utility wrapper that throws an exception if the request returns an error.

classes.name: freighter.unary.UnaryClient

methods.name: freighter.unary.UnaryClient.send
documentation:
Sends a request to the target server and waits until a response is
returned.

```
param target

the target address of the server

param req

the request to issue to the server

param res_t

the response type expected from the server. Implementations can use
```

this to validate the response.
\:return: any errors encountered
\:raises Unreachable: when the provided target cannot be reached

Protocol for an entity that implements a simple request-response transport
between two entities.

classes.name: freighter.unary.AsyncUnaryClient

methods.name: freighter.unary.AsyncUnaryClient.send
documentation:
Sends a request to the target server and waits until a response is
returned.

```
param target

the target address of the server

param req

the request to issue to the server

param res_t

the response from the server. Implementations can use
```

this to validate the response.
\:return: any errors encountered
\:raises Unreachable: when the provided target cannot be reached

Protocol for an entity that implements a simple asynchronous request-response
transport between two entities.

"Middleware is a general middleware function that can be used to parse/attach

variables.name: freighter.transport.Finalizer
documentation:
Finalizer is a middleware that is executed as the last step in a chain.
It is used to finalize the request and return the response.

documentation:
Finalizer is a middleware that is executed as the last step in a chain.
It is used to finalize the request and return the response.

classes.name: freighter.transport.MiddlewareCollector

methods.name: freighter.transport.MiddlewareCollector.use
documentation: Use implements the Transport protocol.

methods.name: freighter.transport.MiddlewareCollector.exec
documentation:
Executes the middleware in order, passing metadata to each
middleware until the end of the chain is reached. It then calls
the finalizer with the metadata.

```
param ctx

the context to pass to the middleware.

param finalizer

the finalizer to call at the end of the chain.
```
MiddlewareCollector collects and executes middleware in order.

documentation: Use implements the Transport protocol.

Executes the middleware in order, passing metadata to each
middleware until the end of the chain is reached. It then calls
the finalizer with the metadata.

```
param md

the metadata to pass to the middleware

param finalizer

the finalizer to call at the end of the chain
```
AsyncMiddlewareCollector collects and executes middleware in order.

classes.name: freighter.stream.AsyncStreamReceiver

methods.name: freighter.stream.AsyncStreamReceiver.receive
documentation:
Receives a response from the stream. It's not safe to call receive concurrently.

```
returns freighter.errors.EOF

if the server closed the stream nominally.

returns Exception

if the server closed the stream abnormally,
```

returns the error the server returned.
\:raises Exception: if the transport fails.
Protocol for an entity that receives a stream of response asynchronously.

classes.name: freighter.stream.StreamReceiver

methods.name: freighter.stream.StreamReceiver.receive
documentation:
Receives a response from the stream. It's not safe to call receive concurrently.

```
param timeout

the maximum amount of time to wait for a response. If None, the
```

method will block indefinitely. Not all implementations support this parameter.

```
returns freighter.errors.EOF

if the server closed the stream nominally.

returns Exception

if the server closed the stream abnormally,
```

returns the error the server returned.
\:raises Exception: if the transport fails.

methods.name: freighter.stream.StreamReceiver.received
documentation: Returns True if the stream has received a response.
Protocol for an entity that receives a stream of responses.

classes.name: freighter.stream.AsyncStreamSender

methods.name: freighter.stream.AsyncStreamSender.send
documentation:
Sends a request to the stream. It is not safe to call send concurrently
with close_send or send.

```
param request

the request to send.

returns freighter.errors.EOF

if the server closed the stream. The caller
```

can discover the error returned by the server by calling receive().
\:returns None: if the message was sent successfully.
\:raises freighter.errors.StreamClosed: if the client called close_send()
\:raises Exception: if the transport fails.
Protocol for an entity that asynchronously sends a stream of requests.

classes.name: freighter.stream.StreamSender

methods.name: freighter.stream.StreamSender.send
documentation:
Sends a request to the stream. It is not safe to call send concurrently
with close_send or send.

```
param request

the request to send.

returns freighter.errors.EOF

if the server closed the stream. The caller
```

can discover the error returned by the server by calling receive().
\:returns None: if the message was sent successfully.
\:raises freighter.errors.StreamClosed: if the client called close_send()
\:raises Exception: if the transport fails.
Protocol for an entity that sends a stream of requests.

classes.name: freighter.stream.AsyncStreamSenderCloser

methods.name: freighter.stream.AsyncStreamSenderCloser.close_send
documentation:
Lets the server know no more messages will be sent. If the client attempts
to call send() after calling close_send(), a freighter.errors.StreamClosed
exception will be raised. close_send is idempotent. If the server has
already closed the stream, close_send will do nothing.

After calling close_send, the client is responsible for calling receive()
to successfully receive the server's acknowledgement.

```
return

None
```

An extension of the AsyncStreamSender protocol that allows the client to
asynchronously close the sending direction of the stream when finished issuing
requests.

classes.name: freighter.stream.StreamSenderCloser

methods.name: freighter.stream.StreamSenderCloser.close_send
documentation:
Lets the server know no more messages will be sent. If the client attempts
to call send() after calling close_send(), a freighter.errors.StreamClosed
exception will be raised. close_send is idempotent. If the server has
already closed the stream, close_send will do nothing.

After calling close_send, the client is responsible for calling receive()
to successfully receive the server's acknowledgement.

```
return

None
```

An extension of the StreamSender protocol that allows the client to
close the sending direction of the stream when finished issuing requests.

classes.name: freighter.stream.AsyncStream
documentation:
Protocol for an entity that asynchronously sends and receives a stream of
requests and responses.

classes.name: freighter.stream.Stream
documentation:
Protocol for an entity that sends and receives a stream of requests and
responses.

classes.name: freighter.stream.AsyncStreamClient

methods.name: freighter.stream.AsyncStreamClient.stream
documentation:
Dials the target and returns a stream that can be used to issue requests
and receive responses.

```
param target

The target to dial. In some implementations, this may be an endpoint
```

path, or in others, a complete hostname or URL.
\:param req_t: The type of the request being issues. This is used to type check
outgoing requests.
\:param res_t: The type of the response being received. This is used to type check
incoming responses.
\:returns: A stream that can be used to issue requests and receive responses.

Protocol for an entity that asynchronously sends and receives a stream of
requests and responses from a server.

classes.name: freighter.stream.StreamClient

methods.name: freighter.stream.StreamClient.stream
documentation:
Dials the target and returns an open stream that can be used to issue requests
and receive responses.

```
param target

The target to dial. In some implementations, this may be an endpoint
```

path, or in others, a complete hostname or URL.
\:param req_t: The type of the request being issues. This is used to type check
outgoing requests.
\:param res_t: The type of the response being received. This is used to type check
incoming responses.
\:returns: An open stream that can be used to issue requests and receive responses.

Protocol for an entity that synchronously sends and receives a stream of requests and
responses from a server.
